9. Make sure the EDI-PI (10-digit) cert is being presented, not the legacy FASC-N (16-digit) cert.
10. If you got a new CAC after June 11, 2016 and your account is one of the legacy types, ask for a fresh standard account - DISA had Root CA3/CA4 issues with the old type.

title: Clear old / stale CAC certificates from Internet Explorer
symptoms: Browser keeps picking an old cert from a prior CAC.
causes: Personal certificate store still holds the old certs.
fix:
1. IE > Tools > Internet Options > Content > Certificates > Personal tab.
2. Select expired / unwanted entries and Remove.
3. Repeat for Other People and Intermediate Certification Authorities if needed.
4. Eject and reinsert CAC to repopulate.

title: Clear stale certs with Cert_Cleanup.bat
symptoms: Cannot access certain CAC sites due to leftover bad certs after multiple CAC issuances.
causes: Old certs in the personal store and intermediate store interfere with the current ones.
fix:
1. Download Cert_Cleanup.bat from militarycac.com (rename from .txt if the browser blocks .bat).
2. Right-click > Run as administrator.
3. Reboot.

title: Constant PIN prompts using Windows native smart card stack
symptoms: Windows asks for the CAC PIN on every CAC site.
causes: Windows native smart card stack does not cache PINs the way ActivClient does.
fix:
1. Install ActivID 7.1.0.153 or newer - it caches the PIN for ~15 minutes.
2. Reboot.

title: DoD Civilians cannot find ActivClient to download
symptoms: DoD civilian employee cannot locate the ActivClient installer because there is no centralized civilian download.
causes: Distribution is delegated to each branch and to individual agencies.
fix:
1. Identify your hosting branch/agency.
2. Use that branch's CAC software portal (Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines, Coast Guard each have separate pages linked from militarycac.com).
3. If your agency does not host it, purchase a license from SCB Solutions (ActivClient 7.4) via the link on militarycac.com/activclient.htm.

title: DTS / DBSign stalls at 'Loading cryptographic libraries'
symptoms: DTS login hangs at the DBSign loading bar or returns a security info error.
causes: *.osd.mil is not in Trusted Sites, or Protected Mode is on.
fix:
1. IE > Tools > Internet Options > Security > Trusted sites > Sites.
2. Add *.osd.mil. Uncheck 'Require server verification (https:)'. Click Add > Close > OK.
3. Back on the Security tab, select Internet (globe) > uncheck 'Enable Protected Mode' > Custom level.
4. Restart IE.

title: DTS will not authenticate on 64-bit Vista or Windows 7
symptoms: DTS shows the login page but the user cannot proceed.
causes: 64-bit Internet Explorer cannot run the DTS authentication ActiveX/Java pieces.
fix:
1. Open C:\Program Files (x86)\Internet Explorer\.
2. Double-click iexplore.exe (the ~622KB 32-bit copy).
3. Pin or shortcut that 32-bit IE to your desktop.
4. Use it for DTS.

title: Error 1920 'Service Smart Card failed to start'
symptoms: Setup fails to start the Smart Card service.
causes: SCardSvr registry permissions are damaged so LOCAL SERVICE cannot start it.
fix: See the broader Smart Card service fix - register the LOCAL SERVICE account against HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais and grant Query/Set/Create/Notify/Delete permissions. The packaged registry-fix .zip from militarycac.com automates it.

title: Government computer will not read the CAC after the lock screen
symptoms: Workstation cannot see the CAC after it locks; only a reboot fixes it.
causes: Aero theme or registry permission causes buffer-overflow / write errors on resume.
fix:
1. Turn off Windows Aero theme (right-click desktop > Personalize > Basic theme).
2. As a stopgap, unplug and replug the reader (or USB keyboard with built-in reader) when the lock screen does not see the card.

title: Install Error 1321 'insufficient privileges to modify the file'
symptoms: Setup fails saying it cannot write to C:\Program Files\...
causes: Not running elevated, or Windows file ACLs are damaged.
fix:
1. Right-click the installer > Run as administrator.
2. If still failing, take ownership of the target Program Files folder (Properties > Security > Advanced > Owner > Administrators > Replace owner on subcontainers) and retry.

title: Install Error 1324 'The path contains an invalid character'
symptoms: Setup aborts complaining about an invalid character in the path.
causes: Shell folder registry values point at a path with illegal characters (often an old roaming-profile redirection).
fix:
1. Create a brand-new local Windows user account.
2. Log in as that account and install ActivClient.
3. Migrate data into the new profile.
4. For the underlying fix see windowsxp.mvps.org/usershellfolders.htm.

title: Install Error 1327 'Invalid Drive X'
symptoms: Setup says 'Invalid Drive X' for some drive letter that is not C.
causes: User Shell Folders registry values reference a drive that no longer exists (e.g. an old network drive H:).
fix:
1. Win+R > regedit.
2. Go to HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders.
3. Find any value whose data starts with a drive other than C: and edit it back to C:\.
4. Retry install.
References: support.microsoft.com/kb/327763, error1327.net.

title: Install Error 1500 'Another installation is in progress'
symptoms: ActivClient setup refuses to start because Windows Installer thinks another install is running.
causes: Stale msiserver state.
fix:
1. Reboot and try again.
2. If still failing: Right-click This PC > Manage (as admin) > Services and Applications > Services > Windows Installer.
3. Set Startup type to Disabled. Apply. Reboot.
4. Reopen Services and set Windows Installer back to Manual. Apply.
5. Retry install.

title: Install Error 1722 'A program run as part of the setup did not finish as expected'
symptoms: Setup runs partway and then errors with 1722.
causes: An install script aborted, usually because of antivirus interference or a profile-specific permission problem.
fix:
1. Click OK to dismiss.
2. Run Windows Update so the Windows Installer service is current.
3. Temporarily disable antivirus and firewall.
4. Right-click the installer > Run as administrator.
5. If it still fails, create a new local user profile and install from there.
6. As a last resort, reinstall Windows.

title: Install Error 2738 'Could not access VBScript run time for custom action'
symptoms: ActivClient installer fails with Error 2738.
causes: vbscript.dll is not registered, or an antivirus is blocking VBScript.
fix:
1. Start > type cmd > right-click Command Prompt > Run as administrator.
2. 32-bit Windows: cd \windows\system32. 64-bit Windows: cd \windows\syswow64.
3. Run: regsvr32 vbscript.dll. You should see 'DllRegisterServer in vbscript.dll succeeded'.
4. If that does not work, in regedit delete HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID\{B54F3741-5B07-11CF-A4B0-00AA004A55E8} (32-bit) or the same key under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (64-bit), taking ownership first if needed. Reboot.
5. Also try temporarily disabling McAfee, Symantec/Norton, or Kaspersky during install; use the official McAfee removal tool if McAfee is the culprit.

title: Install Error 2755 'Server returned unexpected error 110'
symptoms: Installer fails citing the .msi cannot be opened.
causes: Encrypted File System (EFS) on the source directory blocks Windows Installer from reading the package.
fix:
1. Copy the installer to an unencrypted FAT32 USB drive.
2. Run setup from the USB.

title: Set Adobe Reader as the default PDF handler
symptoms: PDFs open in Edge or the built-in viewer and you cannot sign them.
causes: Edge/built-in viewers do not support CAC signing.
fix:
Windows 10:
1. Search > 'default'.
2. Default Programs > 'Associate a file type or protocol with a program'.
3. Find .pdf > Change program > Adobe Acrobat Reader.
Windows 11:
1. Save the PDF locally.
2. Settings > Apps > Default apps > search '.pdf'.
3. Set Adobe Acrobat Reader.

title: Use System Restore when nothing else fixes the CAC
symptoms: Persistent CAC failure after recent changes to the system.
causes: Recent Windows update, third-party install, or driver change corrupted CAC functionality.
fix:
1. Start > All Programs > Accessories > System Tools > System Restore.
2. Pick a restore point from before the problem.
3. Next > Next > restart.

title: Windows 10/11 hides the Authentication cert behind 'More choices'
symptoms: Certificate picker only shows the EMAIL cert; the PIV/Authentication cert is missing.
causes: Windows 10/11 hides additional certs behind 'More choices'.
fix:
1. In the certificate dialog, click 'More choices'.
2. Pick the certificate WITHOUT 'EMAIL' in its name (Authentication / Smart Card Logon).
3. Click OK.

title: Wrong program opens .zip files
symptoms: .zip files open in the wrong application after installing ActivClient or related software.
causes: File association overridden during install.
fix:
Vista/7/8/10/11:
1. Win+R > cmd > run as administrator.
2. Run: assoc .zip=CompressedFolder.
Alternative:
1. Right-click any .zip > Open with > Choose another app.
2. Browse C:\Windows\explorer.exe.
XP:
1. My Computer > Tools > Folder Options > File Types.
2. Scroll to ZIP > Change.
3. Pick 'Compressed (zipped) Folders' > OK.
